summary: Implemented deterministic M4 campaign withdrawal records, campaign result accounting, and WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate handoff DTOs for the accepted M3 postwar governance boundary without applying M3 governance directly.

tests_run.result: 21 files and 151 tests passed, including M4 withdrawal and postwar handoff scenarios.
1 file and 8 tests passed, including withdrawal command/query schema coverage.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched; command/query hash d275faa4; save/load hash 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 32 root Vitest files and 218 tests passed; web 1 file and 3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001 and REVIEW handoff JSON
tests_run.result: Task JSON and REVIEW handoff JSON parsed successfully with expected status, route, required keys, and tests_run shape.

decisions: Withdrawal is resolved through the shared GameCommand protocol surface and records orderly withdrawal, forced retreat, cancellation before departure, or failed extraction.
Objective-complete withdrawal creates explicit WarOutcome and PostwarCandidate DTO state for M3 preview/application callers; it does not bypass or apply M3 governance validation.
Forced retreat and failed extraction paths retain human and political costs through casualty accounting, supply loss, campaign reason codes, and reputation/credit hooks.

No tactical pursuit, naval evacuation, full diplomacy, peace treaty, rebellion, campaign planning UI, formal historical content, M5 content, telemetry, server, multiplayer, arbitrary-code mod, or save-format package work was added.
risks: R3: authoritative M4 state, protocol command/query schema, and canonical hashing changed; systems_architect review is required.
The save-format package was intentionally untouched per forbidden paths; reviewers should confirm the sim-core-only DTO/state shape is the correct boundary for this milestone.
requested_action: Review the M4 withdrawal, campaign result, and M3 postwar candidate handoff boundaries; ACCEPT or REQUEST_CHANGES.
